matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes (3-d shapes) grade level mathematics students cube cuboid (rectangular prism) right circular cylinder right circular cone sphere hemisphere surface areas and volumes: 3d shapes introduction to surface area and volume mathematics: surface areas & volumes focus: cubes, cylinders, cones, spheres defining surface area and volume surface area definition volume definition total surface area (tsa): sum of areas of all faces. curved surface area (csa): only the curved part's area. volume: the amount of space occupied by a solid. the cube: tsa, csa, and volume cube formulas side length = 'a' tsa = sum of 6 squares = 6a² csa = area of 4 side faces = 4a² volume = a × a × a = a³ the cuboid (rectangular prism) cuboid formulas dimensions: length (l), breadth (b), height (h) tsa = 2(lb + bh + hl) sq. units csa = 2h(l + b) sq. units volume = l × b × h cubic units right circular cylinder formulas cylinder tsa and csa radius = r, height = h curved surface area (csa) = 2πrh total surface area (tsa) = 2πr(r + h) volume = πr²h right circular cone formulas cone tsa and csa radius (r), height (h), slant height (l) pythagoras: l² = r² + h² csa = πrl sq. units tsa = πr(r + l) s...
